title: In production, which file actually gets loaded?
summary: Google for Developers presents a short-form video exploring how package imports can behave differently in production versus development environments, despite identical application code and package names. The video highlights a common source of confusion in software deployment where the same import statement loads different files depending on the deployment context.
writeup: This Google for Developers clip poses a question about package imports: in production, which file actually gets loaded when an application references a given package name. It illustrates a discrepancy where identical application code and package names can produce different runtime behavior after deployment. The takeaway centers on dependency resolution and how production environments can load a different file than expected, with implications for package management and reliable deployment.
